name: NautilusTrader (High-Performance Algo Trading)

auth.notes: Exchange API keys per adapter; Interactive Brokers, Binance, Bybit, etc.

performance.notes: Rust core with Python API; sub-millisecond message passing; suitable for HFT

agent_readiness.notes: Production-grade algo trading with Rust core. Strategy class: on_bar(), on_trade(), on_order(). Backtesting engine with identical code path to live trading. Adapters: Interactive Brokers, Binance, Bybit, Databento, Betfair. Actor model for concurrency. Persistence via MessageBus. Catalog for historical data. Risk engine built-in. Complex but feature-complete for institutional use. Much steeper learning curve than Freqtrade but far more capable.

gotchas: Very steep learning curve — complex architecture
Minimal beginner documentation; assumes trading systems knowledge
Rust compilation required for some components
LGPL-3.0 has implications for commercial use
best_when: When backtest-to-live code parity is critical
Teams needing Rust-core performance in Python ecosystem
avoid_when: Retail traders or quant beginners (Freqtrade far simpler)
Simple strategy backtesting (vectorbt or Zipline better)
When millisecond latency is not required
summary: NautilusTrader is a production-grade algo trading platform with Rust core and Python API. Sub-millisecond message passing, identical backtest/live code paths. Adapters for IB, Binance, Bybit, Databento. Built-in risk engine. Complex Actor model architecture. Best for institutional HFT and professional algorithmic trading. Steep learning curve — use Freqtrade for retail crypto bots.
